Art title 1: Station 5 Jesus is Judged by Pilot
Artist Statement 1: Pilate stands across from Jesus, having shed his robe. So now bothare nearly naked showing that in a way they were the same, kingnaked before king, one with earthly power, the other claiming evenmore power. Meanwhile religious leaders cry out accusations againstJesus about which Pilate must soon conclude a verdict. Pilate's wifecautions him from around the corner because of a dream she had.She says, have nothing to do with that innocent man. The text inthese mandalas are often backwards, because I want your eye to haveto wander and search out where the same text is forward andreadable. I want this to force you to explore all the facets of eachmandala, as an aid to draw you into the situation and meditate onthe events and emotions the characters were feeling. I have takenliberties when reinterpreting the actual words of the text to help ushear it in new ways, often in more colloquial language in a way wemight communicate it today.
Story Title again: ANIMATION: Station 5 Jesus is Judged by Pilot
excerpt:
(See video below) The chief priests with the elders and the scribes, that is, the whole Sanhedrin, held a council. They bound Jesus, led him away, and handed him over to Pilate. Pilate questioned him, "Are you the king of the Jews?" He said to him in reply, "You say so." The chief priests accused him of many things. Again Pilate questioned him, "Have you no answer? See how many things they accuse you of." Jesus gave him no further answer, so that Pilate was amazed. . . . Pilate, wishing to satisfy the crowd, released Barrabas . . . and handed Jesus over to be crucified.
(Mark 15: 1--5, 15, NAB)

Lord, grant us discernment, that we may
see as you see, not as the world sees.


Think of the mass of people trying to condemn Jesus. When have you felt like the object of
scorn or accusation? Have people at your work or at school, or even at home, turned others
against you?

Jesus felt like that too.
Look at Jesus, standing before the powerful Pilate, Jesus's life or death hanging on what Pilate will decide about him. When have you been before someone important? Did you change who you are to make them think more of you or to direct your fate? Does Jesus here, in some way, give an example of how we should respond in situations like that? What was at the heart of his response (or lack of response)?
Perhaps Pilate, amazed at someone who could keep silent through such accusations, was trying to say, "What type of place are you from that you do not answer their accusations?" Perhaps he thought, "What type of kingdom's residents have such security in their future?"
Then he hears that Jesus claims to be a king and maybe he means to ask, "Wait, you are a king as well? Do you ... do you come to us on behalf of that kingdom?" He suddenly finds himself before a man who might hold a position of much more power than himself.
If you found yourself in this moment, a potential king of a heavenly kingdom before you, and the much louder earthly voices telling you it's not true and to unjustly condemn him, what might you think, say, and do?

Pilate may have been trying to tell Jesus, "Don't you know the path ahead depends on you
answering me?" Jesus had a very hard journey ahead of him. Have you had a hard task ahead
 of you when there is an easy way out if you just change the truth a little bit? If you were to just
say a little lie? With your taxes or finances? With your partner? With your boss? Jesus felt this.

How might this be an area you can bring into more alignment with God's desire for you?

Have you been like Pilate's wife? Do you feel like God has given you dreams or signs? What are 
they? And have you taken appropriate action on them?


When have you felt pressure to do something that you didn't feel comfortable with? But your
 reputation, your career advancement, or the opinion of others swayed you to do it anyway?
Jesus felt this. What was it, and how would you do it differently now?


Is God calling you to do something hard? Maybe that would be standing up for someone who
has been shamed or staying true to your own hard journey ahead? Jesus dealt with this decision
too.


Take heart for the kingdoms of this world are not as strong as, nor will they last as long as, the 
kingdom of which we are true residents and to where we will be going home someday.

Amen.
